title: Throat swelling after eating at a restaurant
presentation: A 23-year-old female with a known peanut allergy presents via EMS 15 minutes after eating at a restaurant. She has throat tightness, hives over her trunk, and difficulty swallowing. EMS gave no medications. Vitals: BP 88/52, HR 132, RR 28, Temp 37.0C, SpO2 93%. Exam: stridor, diffuse urticaria, lip and tongue swelling, diffuse wheeze.

urgent: repeat epinephrine if no improvement in 5-15 min
gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Epinephrine IM is the FIRST and most critical intervention — never delay it. Antihistamines and steroids are adjuncts only. Biphasic reaction can occur 8-72 hours later — observe for minimum 4-6 hours.
